perturbation.original_text: As used in this Agreement, the term “Confidential Information” means all oral and written information furnished by, or on behalf of, PHCS to the Recipient or its Representatives (as defined below), which is not ge nerally known to the public, including, without limitation, all trade secrets, products, procedures, manuals, guidelines, reports, communic ations, information regarding names and requirements of current and prospective suppliers, vendors and customers, tec hnical information regarding current and prospective products and programs and the development and/or acquisition of future products, programs a nd enhancements, discoveries, concepts and ideas, nature and results of research and development activities, tec hnical information on product or program performance and reliability, processes, formulae, techniques, “know -how,” specifications, any “protected health information” as defined in HIPAA regulations, 45 C.F.R. Parts 160 and 164, as amended from time to time , and any other material or information related to the business or activities of PHCS, whether or not protectible by patent, copyright or other laws, whe ther furnished before or after the date of this Agreement, together with any additional information der ived from such information and contained in all analyses, compilations, studies or other documents or records prepared by the Recipient or its Representatives, as well as al l copies and other reproductions thereof, whether in writing or stored or maintaine d in or by electronic, magnetic or other means, media or devices. As used in this Agreement, the term “Representatives” means, with respect to PHCS or the Recipient, their directors , officers, shareholders, members, managers, partners, employees, affiliat es and controlling persons, and their agents, advisors and representatives (including without limitation attorneys, accountants and financial advisors).
perturbation.changed_text: As used in this Agreement, the term “Confidential Information” means information deemed confidential by PHCS. As used in this Agreement, the term “Representatives” means, with respect to PHCS or the Recipient, their directors , officers, shareholders, members, managers, partners, employees, affiliat es and controlling persons, and their agents, advisors and representatives (including without limitation attorneys, accountants and financial advisors).
perturbation.explanation: By removing the detailed description of 'Confidential Information' and replacing it with a vague 'information deemed confidential by PHCS,' the definition becomes subjective. Later sections that rely on a clear definition of 'Confidential Information' for determining obligations (e.g., permitted use, return, compelled disclosure) now lack a concrete basis, creating uncertainty and in-text contradiction. For example, the destruction clause relies on a specific understanding of what constitutes confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Destruction/Return of Confiden tial Information . Upon PHCS’ request, all Confidential Information (and all copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part thereof), whether in writing or stored or maintained in or by electronic, magnetic or other means, media or devices, sh all be returned or destroyed (such destruction to be certified in writing) by an authorized officer and not retained in any form or for any reason.
perturbation.justification: The original text provides a specific definition of 'Confidential Information', whereas the changed text replaces it with a vague and subjective definition: 'information deemed confidential by PHCS.' This creates a contradiction with the 'Destruction/Return of Confidential Information' clause. The clause relies on a clear, objective understanding of what constitutes 'Confidential Information' for determining obligations (destruction of confidential information), making the obligation unclear and potentially unenforceable, since PHCS can define it at will.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in Parag raph 1, Confidential Information does not include information which (i) was or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by the Recipient or its Representatives; or (ii) was or becomes available to the Recipient on a non -confidential basis from a source other than PHCS, provided that such source is not bound by a confidentiality agreement with PHCS or otherwise prohibited from transmitti ng the information to the Recipient; or (iii) was within the Recipient’s possession prior to its being furnished by or on behalf of PHCS, provided that the source of such information was not bound by a confidentiality agreement with PHCS in respect thereof or otherwise pr ohibited from transmitt ing the information to the Recipient.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in Parag raph 1, Confidential Information does not include information which (i) was or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by the Recipient or its Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting clauses (ii) and (iii) which provided further exceptions to what constitutes confidential information, we create a contradiction. Paragraph 1 now broadly defines confidential information but paragraph 2 now only considers what becomes publicly available, omitting other criteria. This raises an in-text contradiction regarding the full scope of exceptions allowed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Definition of Confidential Information . As used in this Agreement, the term “Confidential Information” means all oral and written information furnished by, or on behalf of, PHCS to the Recipient or its Representatives (as defined below), which is not ge nerally known to the public, including, without limitation, all trade secrets, products, procedures, manuals, guidelines, reports, communic ations, information regarding names and requirements of current and prospective suppliers, vendors and customers, tec hnical information regarding current and prospective products and programs and the development and/or acquisition of future products, programs a nd enhancements, discoveries, concepts and ideas, nature and results of research and development activities, tec hnical information on product or program performance and reliability, processes, formulae, techniques, “know -how,” specifications, any “protected health information” as defined in HIPAA regulations, 45 C.F.R. Parts 160 and 164, as amended from time to time , and any other material or information related to the business or activities of PHCS, whether or not protectible by patent, copyright or other laws, whe ther furnished before or after the date of this Agreement, together with any additional information der ived from such information and contained in all analyses, compilations, studies or other documents or records prepared by the Recipient or its Representatives, as well as al l copies and other reproductions thereof, whether in writing or stored or maintaine d in or by electronic, magnetic or other means, media or devices. As used in this Agreement, the term “Representatives” means, with respect to PHCS or the Recipient, their directors , officers, shareholders, members, managers, partners, employees, affiliat es and controlling persons, and their agents, advisors and representatives (including without limitation attorneys, accountants and financial advisors).
perturbation.justification: The original text in Paragraph 2 provides three exceptions to what constitutes confidential information. The changed text omits two of these exceptions, significantly narrowing the scope of what is *not* considered confidential. This creates a direct contradiction with the 'Definition of Confidential Information' which broadly defines it. By eliminating the exceptions (ii) and (iii) from paragraph 2, the changed text implies that any information not publicly available is confidential, conflicting with the original understanding that certain information from other sources or prior possession wouldn't be considered confidential. The explanation confirms this contradiction, stating that the changed text contradicts the original agreement regarding the full scope of exceptions allowed. Therefore, there is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: As a condition to the disclosure of the Confidential Information, the Recipient agrees to keep the Confidential Information confidential and not to disclose or otherwise use the Confi dential Information for any purpose, other than for its business relationship with PHCS, without the prior written consent of PHCS. Accordingly, the Recipient agrees to treat the C onfidential Information which it receives as it would its own confidential information and to take all reasonable precautions to prevent the unauthorized disclosure to any third party of any Confidential Information. The Recipient further agrees to disclose the Con fidential Information only to those Representatives who need to k now such information for the purpose of its business relationship with PHCS and who shall (i) be advised by the Recipient of this Agreement and the confidential and non -public nature of the Confidential Information, and (ii) agree with the Recipient to be bound by the provisions hereof. The Recipient shall be responsible for any improper use of the Confidential Information by its Representatives and agrees, at its own expense, to take all reasonable measures, i ncluding but not limited to court proceedings, to restrain its Representatives from unauthorized disclosure or use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: As a condition to the disclosure of the Confidential Information, the Recipient agrees to keep the Confidential Information confidential and not to disclose or otherwise use the Confi dential Information for any purpose, other than for its business relationship with PHCS, without the prior written consent of PHCS. Accordingly, the Recipient agrees to treat the C onfidential Information which it receives with reasonable care to prevent disclosure to any third party of any Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause that specifies the recipient must disclose confidential information only to representatives that have been advised of the agreement and bound to its terms, and that the recipient is responsible for its representative's use of confidential information, creates an in-text contradiction. This now implies the recipient has no specific obligation to ensure their representatives also adhere to the confidentiality agreement. This conflicts with the general intent of the agreement, creating ambiguity and impacting enforceability, particularly around third-party disclosures.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Indemnification . The Recipient agrees to indemnify and hold harmless PHCS, and its aff iliates, and its and their shareholders, directors, officers, members, managers, partners, employees and agents (collectively, the “Indemnified Parties”) from and against any an d all costs and expenses (including without limitation reasonable attorneys’ fe es) incurred by or on behalf of any Indemnified Party arising out of any breach of any provision of this Agreement by the Recipient or its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the Recipient is responsible for the actions of its Representatives regarding confidential information. The changed text removes this obligation, stating only that the Recipient must treat the information with reasonable care. This omission contradicts the Indemnification clause, which holds the Recipient liable for breaches of the agreement by its Representatives. By removing the specific duty to ensure Representatives are bound by the agreement, it creates a direct contradiction in that the Recipient is now being indemnified for something they no longer have explicit control over.

perturbation.original_text: In addition, without the prior written consent of PHCS, except to the extent Confidential Information may be disclosed to Representatives of the Recipien t or pursuant to the exception identified in Paragraph 7, neither the Recipient nor its Representatives will disclose to any person (which shall be broadly interpreted to include, without limitation, any corporat ion company, group, partnership, trust, association or individual) (i) that the Confidential Information has been made available to them, (ii) that they have inspected any portion thereof, (iii) the existence of, or any of the terms, conditions or other fa cts with respect to, this Agreement or its business relationship with PHCS.
perturbation.changed_text: In addition, without the prior written consent of PHCS, neither the Recipient nor its Representatives will disclose to any person (which shall be broadly interpreted to include, without limitation, any corporat ion company, group, partnership, trust, association or individual) (i) that the Confidential Information has been made available to them, (ii) that they have inspected any portion thereof, (iii) the existence of, or any of the terms, conditions or other fa cts with respect to, this Agreement or its business relationship with PHCS.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause that allows disclosure to representatives or pursuant to paragraph 7, we now have an in-text contradiction, in that there is no mention to the Representatives anymore, which now creates a broad in-text contradiction for the entire text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Use of Confidential Information . As a condition to the disclosure of the Confidential Information, the Recipient agrees to keep the Confidential Information confidential and not to disclose or otherwise use the Confi dential Information for any purpose, other than for its business relationship with PHCS, without the prior written consent of PHCS. Accordingly, the Recipient agrees to treat the C onfidential Information which it receives as it would its own confidential information and to take all reasonable precautions to prevent the unauthorized disclosure to any third party of any Confidential Information. The Recipient further agrees to disclose the Con fidential Information only to those Representatives who need to k now such information for the purpose of its business relationship with PHCS and who shall (i) be advised by the Recipient of this Agreement and the confidential and non -public nature of the Confidential Information, and (ii) agree with the Recipient to be bound by the provisions hereof. The Recipient shall be responsible for any improper use of the Confidential Information by its Representatives and agrees, at its own expense, to take all reasonable measures, i ncluding but not limited to court proceedings, to restrain its Representatives from unauthorized disclosure or use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows disclosure of confidential information to representatives or pursuant to paragraph 7. The changed text removes this allowance. The contradicted text specifies that the recipient may disclose confidential information to representatives under certain conditions. The change therefore creates a direct contradiction because the changed text now prohibits something that the contradicted text explicitly allows, creating an in-text contradiction as per the definition 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Neither PHCS nor its Representatives make any representations or warranties as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information. Each Recipient agree s that neither PHCS nor any of its Representatives shall have any liability to the Recipient or its Representatives resulting from the use of the Confidential Information supplied by PHCS or any of its Repres entatives.
perturbation.changed_text: Neither PHCS nor its Representatives make any representations or warranties as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information. Each Recipient agree s that neither PHCS nor any of its Representatives shall have any liability to the Recipient resulting from the use of the Confidential Information supplied by PHCS or any of its Repres entatives.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the liability exclusion of PHCS towards representatives of the receiver of the confidential information, it creates an in-text contradiction. Earlier the text included the Representatives, excluding them from any harm, now this is not a factor anymore, but they are still mentioned in the earlier clauses of the document, creating this in-text contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: Use of Confidential Information . As a condition to the disclosure of the Confidential Information, the Recipient agrees to keep the Confidential Information confidential and not to disclose or otherwise use the Confi dential Information for any purpose, other than for its business relationship with PHCS, without the prior written consent of PHCS. Accordingly, the Recipient agrees to treat the C onfidential Information which it receives as it would its own confidential information and to take all reasonable precautions to prevent the unauthorized disclosure to any third party of any Confidential Information. The Recipient further agrees to disclose the Con fidential Information only to those Representatives who need to k now such information for the purpose of its business relationship with PHCS and who shall (i) be advised by the Recipient of this Agreement and the confidential and non -public nature of the Confidential Information, and (ii) agree with the Recipient to be bound by the provisions hereof. The Recipient shall be responsible for any improper use of the Confidential Information by its Representatives and agrees, at its own expense, to take all reasonable measures, i ncluding but not limited to court proceedings, to restrain its Representatives from unauthorized disclosure or use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text excluded liability to the Recipient and its Representatives. The changed text removes the exclusion of liability to the Representatives. The Contradicted Text requires the Recipient to be responsible for the Representatives' use of Confidential Information and obligates the Recipient to restrain them from unauthorized disclosure. By removing the liability exclusion for the Representatives, the changed text now contradicts the clause requiring the Recipient's responsibility over the Representatives, creating a strong in-text contradiction because the Recipient's potential liability for the Representative's actions is now unclear, potentially conflicting with the Recipient's obligation to restrain them. There is now ambiguity where certainty was expected.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary set forth herein, in the event that the Recipient or its Representatives are requested or become legally compelled (by oral questions, interrogatories, request for information or documents, subpoena , civil investigative demand or similar pr ocess) to disclose any of the Confidential Information or take any other action prohibited hereby, the Recipient shall provide PHCS with prompt written notice so that PHCS may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this Agreement. In the event that such protective order or other remedy is not sought or obtained or that the compliance with the provisions of this Agreement is waived, the Recipient agrees that it will furnish on ly that portion of the Confidential Information which, in the reasonable opinion of its counsel, the Recipient is compelled to disclose without stan ding liable for contempt or suffering other censure or penalty. The Recipient further agrees that it will exercise its best effor ts to obtain reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded to that portion of the Confidential Information which subject to the compelled disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary set forth herein, in the event that the Recipient are requested or become legally compelled (by oral questions, interrogatories, request for information or documents, subpoena , civil investigative demand or similar pr ocess) to disclose any of the Confidential Information or take any other action prohibited hereby, the Recipient shall provide PHCS with prompt written notice so that PHCS may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this Agreement. In the event that such protective order or other remedy is not sought or obtained or that the compliance with the provisions of this Agreement is waived, the Recipient agrees that it will furnish on ly that portion of the Confidential Information which, in the reasonable opinion of its counsel, the Recipient is compelled to disclose without stan ding liable for contempt or suffering other censure or penalty. The Recipient further agrees that it will exercise its best effor ts to obtain reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded to that portion of the Confidential Information which subject to the compelled disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase that the 'Representatives are requested', makes the entire text contraditory, because representatives are part of the initial agreement, and their obligations, but if such even happens they are not requested to provide the information in such cases, even though they are part of the agreement, making this clause an internal in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Use of Confidential Information . As a condition to the disclosure of the Confidential Information, the Recipient agrees to keep the Confidential Information confidential and not to disclose or otherwise use the Confi dential Information for any purpose, other than for its business relationship with PHCS, without the prior written consent of PHCS. Accordingly, the Recipient agrees to treat the C onfidential Information which it receives as it would its own confidential information and to take all reasonable precautions to prevent the unauthorized disclosure to any third party of any Confidential Information. The Recipient further agrees to disclose the Con fidential Information only to those Representatives who need to k now such information for the purpose of its business relationship with PHCS and who shall (i) be advised by the Recipient of this Agreement and the confidential and non -public nature of the Confidential Information, and (ii) agree with the Recipient to be bound by the provisions hereof. The Recipient shall be responsible for any improper use of the Confidential Information by its Representatives and agrees, at its own expense, to take all reasonable measures, i ncluding but not limited to court proceedings, to restrain its Representatives from unauthorized disclosure or use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text includes 'Recipient or its Representatives', meaning both are initially bound by the agreement's confidentiality obligations. The changed text removes 'or its Representatives' from the scenario where the Recipient is compelled to disclose information. This creates a contradiction because the 'Contradicted Text' explicitly states that 'The Recipient shall be responsible for any improper use of the Confidential Information by its Representatives' and that the Representatives 'agree with the Recipient to be bound by the provisions hereof.' By removing the representatives from the compelled disclosure clause, the changed text implies they are not bound by the same rules in that specific situation, which directly contradicts their responsibilities and the Recipient's liability for their actions as defined in the 'Contradicted Text', creating an Omissions - In Text Contradiction.
